'''Yang Water Rat''' is the 49th of the sixty nodes of the [[Sexagenary cycle|Divine Sixty]], the great cycle that the [[Heavenly stems|heavenly stems]] and [[Earthly Branches|earthly branches]] trace across the ringworld's calendar of [[Gaia]] and [[Luna]].

Its stem is 壬 Mizunoe (yang water, the gaia-aligned face of winter), and its branch is 子 Ne, the [[Rat (zodiac)|Rat]] hour. Within the [[Wuxing]] reading of the node, both stem and branch share the water element, doubling its weight in this node.

On the [[The divine 60|Divine Sixty]] lattice this position falls between [[Yin Metal Pig]] (node 48) and [[Yin Water Ox]] (node 50).

== Folk reading ==
Yang Water Rat is the inverse of Yang Fire Horse. Where the Horse-year brings fires, the doubled-water Rat-year brings the great flood; the proverb pairs them: ''as Hinoe-uma brings fires, Mizunoe-ne brings the great waters.''

== Stem and branch ==
* '''Stem:''' [[Mizunoe (壬)]] — yang water
* '''Branch:''' [[Rat (zodiac)|Rat (子)]] — yang water
* '''Celestial body:''' [[Gaia]]
* '''Element-season:''' winter

== See also ==
* [[The divine 60]]
* [[Sexagenary cycle]]
* [[Heavenly stems]]
* [[Earthly Branches]]
* [[Yin Metal Pig]] — preceding node
* [[Yin Water Ox]] — following node

{{wikidata link|Q904972}}
[[Category:Sexagenary cycle]]
[[Category:Sexagenary cycle members]]
[[Category:Ringworld]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
